title: ScheduleEvaluator with timer-driven triggers and policy enforcement
purpose: Implement the ScheduleEvaluator that drives schedule execution: arms durable timers for each active schedule's next fire time, evaluates overlap policy when a timer fires, starts workflow executions according to policy, handles catch-up on recovery, and re-arms timers for the next trigger. This is the scheduling heartbeat of the engine.
task: Create aion::schedule::evaluator module with a ScheduleEvaluator that manages active schedules. On creation/resume, it computes next_fire_time and arms a durable timer. When the timer fires, it: (1) checks overlap policy against the current execution (if any), (2) starts a workflow if policy allows, (3) records ScheduleTriggered, (4) computes and arms the next fire time. On recovery, it evaluates catch-up policy for any schedules whose next_fire_time is in the past. Schedule state is projected from events.

requirements.title: Schedule state projection from events
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL maintain per-schedule state projected from schedule events: schedule_id, config, is_paused, is_deleted, last_triggered_at, next_trigger_at, current_execution.
acceptance: After ScheduleCreated, state reflects the initial config with is_paused=false.
After SchedulePaused, is_paused=true; after ScheduleResumed, is_paused=false.

requirements.title: Timer-driven trigger evaluation
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL arm a durable timer for each active schedule. When the timer fires, the evaluator SHALL evaluate the trigger, optionally start a workflow, record ScheduleTriggered, and re-arm. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT use polling.
acceptance: A schedule with a short interval fires within the expected window.
After each trigger, the timer is re-armed for the next fire time.
Paused and deleted schedules do not have armed timers.

requirements.title: Overlap policy enforcement
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL enforce overlap policy when a trigger fires and a previous execution is still running. Skip: do not start. BufferOne: queue one pending. CancelPrevious: cancel running, start new. AllowAll: start regardless.
acceptance: Each of the four overlap policies has a dedicated test.
Skip skips and re-arms; BufferOne queues at most one; CancelPrevious cancels then starts; AllowAll always starts.

requirements.title: Catch-up policy enforcement on recovery
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL evaluate catch-up policy when next_trigger_at is in the past. All: fire every missed trigger. One: fire once immediately. Skip: advance to next future time.
acceptance: Each of the three catch-up policies has a dedicated test.
Catch-up fires are recorded as ScheduleTriggered events.

requirements.title: Recovery reconstructs state and re-arms timers
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL reconstruct all schedule state from events on recovery and re-arm timers for active schedules, applying catch-up policy for past-due triggers.
acceptance: After recovery, active schedules have armed timers.
Paused and deleted schedules are not re-armed.
Catch-up policy is applied before re-arming.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement Engine API methods — that is AK-004.
SHALL NOT implement server endpoints — that is AK-005.
verification: Unit tests cover all four overlap policies and all three catch-up policies.
